commit.message:
Fixes #4809 - Kitty Keyboard Protocol Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model (#4816)

* Start Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model

Mark Phase 2 as in-progress in the kitty keyboard protocol plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Complete Phase 2: Rich keyboard event model with KeyUp, EventType, and ModifierKey

- Add KeyEventType enum (Press/Repeat/Release) and ModifierKey enum for standalone modifier identity
- Extend Key with EventType, ModifierKey, and IsModifierOnly properties (additive, non-breaking)
- Add KeyUp event pipeline: IInputProcessor → IDriver → IKeyboard → Application → View
- Enable kitty flag 2 (report event types) alongside flag 1 (disambiguate escape codes)
- Extract shared ApplyModifiersAndEventType helper in AnsiKeyboardParserPattern base class
- Update CsiKeyPattern and CsiCursorPattern regexes to accept kitty modifiers:event_type format
- Map kitty modifier key codepoints (57358-57451) to ModifierKey enum in KittyKeyboardPattern
- Rename KittyKeyboardPhase1Flags → KittyKeyboardRequestedFlags
- Update Keys scenario to display event type, modifier key identity, and KeyUp events
- Update keyboard.md and drivers.md documentation
- Add 44 new tests (KeyEventTypeTests + KittyKeyboardPhase2Tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix pipeline injection mode and add kitty protocol pipeline tests

Fix two bugs that broke InputInjectionMode.Pipeline:
1. InputProcessorImpl used DateTime.Now instead of _timeProvider.Now for
   stale escape detection, causing immediate release with VirtualTimeProvider
2. AnsiInputProcessor.InjectKeyDownEvent/InjectMouseEvent routed through
   the background input thread; now enqueues directly to InputQueue

Simplify InputInjector.ProcessQueue() by removing the VirtualTimeProvider
60ms-advance workaround (no longer needed with bug #1 fixed).

Un-skip two Pipeline tests and add 27 kitty protocol pipeline tests
covering event types, modifiers, functional keys, standalone modifier
keys, CSI ~/cursor variants, full press-repeat-release cycles, and
negative cases (invalid event types, malformed sequences, recovery).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fixes #4582 - Windows console input encoding for accented chars

Previously, input bytes from the Windows console were always decoded as UTF-8, but ReadFile returns bytes in the console's input code page (e.g., Windows-1252). This caused accented and non-ASCII characters to be misread. This commit introduces a ConsoleInputEncoding property in WindowsVTInputHelper to dynamically use the correct encoding, and updates AnsiInput to use it. Adds unit tests to verify correct decoding of accented characters. Also includes minor test cleanups and improved naming.

* fixed test name

* Fixes link bug that was causing Windows tests to hang.

* Add support for standalone modifier key events (kitty)

Include kitty flag 8 to enable reporting of standalone modifier key events (e.g., Shift, Ctrl, Alt) as escape codes. Update tests to verify correct handling of modifier-only key press and release events, and assert that the requested kitty flags include "report all keys as escape codes." This improves compatibility with terminals supporting the kitty keyboard protocol.

* Update AI agent instructions with comprehensive Copilot guide and new coding conventions

- Replaced .github/copilot-instructions.md stub with full reference covering
  build/test commands, architecture overview, code style, terminology, and
  testing conventions
- Added early return/guard clause convention across all AI instruction files
- Added one-type-per-file convention for public and internal types
- Updated CONTRIBUTING.md (source of truth), CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: use KittyKeyboardFlags enum for protocol flags

Replaces raw int usage with strongly-typed KittyKeyboardFlags enum for kitty keyboard protocol flags throughout the codebase. Adds KittyKeyboardFlags.cs and moves KittyKeyboardProtocolResult to its own file, updating it to use the new enum. Updates all related logic, methods, and tests for improved type safety, clarity, and maintainability.

* Kitty protocol: add alternate key reporting (flag 4)

Adds full support for kitty keyboard protocol "alternate key reporting" (flag 4) in Terminal.Gui. The Key model now exposes ShiftedKeyCode and BaseLayoutKeyCode, populated by the kitty parser for each key event. Updates the parser, documentation, and tests to cover alternate key fields, international layouts, and end-to-end propagation. Replaces old phase 2 tests with new, comprehensive suites for parsing, pipeline, and Key object behavior. Improves operator and parsing logic in Key, and updates docs to guide usage of new features.

* Fix race condition in ResetState Driver disposal

Two threads (test + AppTestHelper background) could race into Dispose() →
ResetState() concurrently. Thread A nulls Driver between Thread B's null-check
and Driver.Dispose(), causing NullReferenceException.

Fix: capture Driver to local, null the property, then unsubscribe and dispose
using the captured local. Concurrent callers see null and skip cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Always download latest install-workiq.ps1 from GitHub

git pull on local clone was failing silently on fresh machines without
git credentials. Now downloads install-workiq.ps1 directly from GitHub
(same pattern as bootstrap downloads install.ps1), falls back to local
copy if download fails.

fix: Skills page — defensive arrays, test queries panel, test cleanup

- Fixed all undefined array accesses (tags, requiredTools, requiredLicenses,
  queries, remediation, detectionRules, applicableWorkflows)
- Aligned Skill interface with SkillPackage API shape (queries have .query
  not .code, remediation has .scripts[] not flat .code)
- Added TestQueriesPanel: run KQL queries against live tenant to verify
  they work before saving. Shows row count per query, run individual or all.

- Increased API timeout for LLM calls in tests (60s for create-from-nl, analyze)

- Add support for subagent.started/completed, assistant.turn_start events

commit.message:
fix(scrape): remove duplicate Playwright implementation causing NameError

The file contained two concatenated implementations. The second (Playwright-
based) redefined scrape_articles/main and added a second __name__ == '__main__'
block, causing sync_playwright NameError on every run. Removed the orphaned
duplicate; the BrightData + requests implementation is authoritative.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(azure-devops): simplify foundation bundle URL to repo root

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: bump version to v1.5.2-beta.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: QA Pass 3 — resolve all 16 findings

Critical:
- C-1: Compute real SHA-256 fingerprint in _ToFUPolicy (was MD5)
  Uses hashlib.sha256(key.asbytes()) + base64; label is now accurate
- C-2: _save_host_key() now schedules _update_status_bar via after(0,...)
  instead of calling it directly from the SSH background thread

High:
- H-1: Remove dead code block in _dpapi_decrypt (CryptProtectData.__class__ check)
- H-2: Remove transport._preferred_keys private attribute access (redundant + fragile)
- H-3: Validate IP/subnet/gateway format in _apply_ip_change before sending command
- H-4: Add TRANSFER_TIMEOUT (300s) on ftp.sock before storbinary() call
- H-5: Reset _current_device/_current_user via self.after(0, _reset) on main thread

Medium:
- M-1: Add timeout=10 to sqlite3.connect() in get_connection()
- M-2: Remove pyinstaller from requirements.txt; add requirements-build.txt
- M-3: Remove macholib wheel from vendor/ (macOS-only, unused on Windows)
- M-4: Rewrite setup.bat shortcut creation using temp .ps1 file to avoid
  empty ICON_LINE being passed as a PowerShell argument
- NEW-1: Use os.path.samefile() in _do_import_database instead of string compare
- NEW-2: Validate imported file is a real SQLite DB before overwriting active DB

Low:
- L-1: MultiInputDialog._confirm() preserves whitespace in password fields
- L-2: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL moved to initialize_db() only (was every connection)
- NEW-3: Firmware on_complete lambda now calls self.after(0, _update_status_bar)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Pull latest before using local clone of gh-copilot-setup

When C:\GitHub\pc-setup already exists, installer used stale local files
for install-workiq.ps1, start.ps1, etc. without ever pulling from GitHub.
Now runs git pull before setting sourceDir so re-runs pick up latest fixes.

feat: onecrawl npm package — binary installer + skills manager

New `packages/onecrawl/` npm package providing:
- `npx onecrawl init` — install AGENTS + 13 skills into any project
- `npx onecrawl update-skills` — update skills to latest version
- `npx onecrawl skills-status` — show installed skills status
- postinstall binary download for 5 platforms (macOS/Linux/Windows)
- All other commands delegate to the Rust CLI binary
- `prepublishOnly` syncs assets from repo root automatically

Usage: npm install onecrawl && npx onecrawl init

- Add RefreshNarratives to TestAdapter with lastResults cache for post-narrative re-enrichment

Fix 400 error: write task to .sarma-task.md, use short CLI prompt

Long multi-line prompts via --prompt cause 400 Bad Request.
Now writes full task details to .sarma-task.md in the worktree
and tells the agent to read it. CLI prompt stays short.

commit.message:
feat: AI Skills system — catalog, NL creator, persistence, analysis injection

New skill system:
- src/lib/skills/index.ts: SkillPackage types, context builder, selector,
  4 built-in skills (credential theft, ransomware, identity hardening, DLP)
- src/lib/db/index.ts: custom_skills table with CRUD functions
- src/app/api/skills/route.ts: list, get, save, delete, create-from-nl
- src/app/skills/page.tsx: Catalog page with cards, filters, NL creator,
  KQL code blocks with copy, remediation scripts, detection rules
- src/lib/agent/workflow-analyzer.ts: Auto-selects relevant skills and
  injects them into analysis context (KQL queries, instructions, scripts)
- Dashboard: 🧠 Skills button in header nav
- Tests: 4 skills tests (list API, page load, NL create/save/delete lifecycle,
  skill injection into analysis)

Skills are automatically matched to workflows by:
- Applicable workflow IDs
- Required tool overlap
- Category mapping
- Tag matching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor: Extract theme resolution helper to eliminate code duplication

Extract duplicated theme resolution logic into a shared prepareDrawContext
helper function. This eliminates 12 lines of duplicated code across Button,
TextInput, Label, and ScrollView Draw methods.

Changes:
- Add prepareDrawContext() helper to resolve position, bounds, and theme
- Add drawContext struct to hold resolved draw parameters
- Refactor Button.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor TextInput.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor Label.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor ScrollView.Draw to use prepareDrawContext

Impact:
- Duplication ratio: 0.32% → 0% (eliminated 12 duplicate lines)
- Code quality score: 100/100 (maintained)
- No behavioral changes
- All tests pass, go vet clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable screen saver and keep-display-on by default (#45)

* Enable screen saver and keep-display-on by default

Most users will want the display to stay on (since the HAP server
can't run in the background) with the screen saver active. Change
both defaults to true and only override from UserDefaults when a
saved value exists, so existing users who explicitly disabled them
keep their preference. Rename the card title to "Keep Display On"
for clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Idempotent annotation to all read-only MCP tools

MCP best practices recommend safety annotations (readOnlyHint,
destructiveHint, idempotent) on all tools. Our read-only tools
were missing Idempotent = true, which signals to clients that
these tools are safe to retry and cache.

CODER: 18.2 — Dedup event handlers + hoist DEFAULT_REPO

Part A: Moved DEFAULT_REPO constant from main.rs to lib.rs as pub const,
updated main.rs to import it, replaced hardcoded strings in event/mod.rs.

Part B: Extracted 5 shared action functions (changelog_open_pr,
changelog_yank_pr, contributions_open_profile, contributions_open_prs,
contributions_yank_url) eliminating duplication between handle_detail_keys
and per-view list handlers.

Part C: Added App::handle_list_click_at() method and collapsed 5-way
ViewMode match in mouse.rs handle_list_click to shared helper with
header guards for Changelog/Board.

Part D: Consolidated mouse.rs scroll handler from 5-way match to use
current_nav_mut() and current_list_len().

All 1980 tests pass, clippy clean, no functional changes.

commit.message:
Fix syntax errors in platform-specific tests and complete validation checklist

- Fixed syntax errors in app_darwin_test.go, app_android_test.go, and app_ios_test.go
  - Removed extra closing braces
  - Added proper error handling for NewWindow calls
- Updated TESTING.md validation checklist to mark all items as complete
  - All 143 unit tests are implemented across 15 test files
  - Tests cover: smoke tests, widget creation, event dispatch, layout resolution, resource loading, and theme propagation
  - go vet passes for Windows target platform

Tests validated:
✅ Unit tests with -race flag (143 test functions)

commit.message:
feat(sse): handle warmup_progress events for statusline

Add support for the new warmup_progress SSE event emitted by SageFs
daemon during session startup.

- Add warmup_progress to SSE event classifier (sse.lua)
- Add SageFsWarmupProgress to event names and map (events.lua)
- Track warmup state (phase, step, total, message, progress) in module
- Show phase-specific warmup label in statusline during startup
- Notify on major phase transitions (not every namespace open)
- Clear warmup state when warmup_context_snapshot arrives (session ready)

commit.message:
feat(daemon): emit warmup_progress SSE events during session startup

Add phase-by-phase warmup progress events so editor plugins can show
detailed status instead of a generic 'warming up...' message.

Daemon changes:
- Add WarmupProgress case to DaemonStateChange DU
- Add formatWarmupProgressEvent to SseWriter with phase derivation
- Extract tryParseWarmupProgress for reuse in SSE broadcast
- Wire warmup callback to trigger both Elm dispatch and SSE broadcast
- Handle WarmupProgress in wireSessionEventSubscription

SSE event format: event: warmup_progress
  data: {SessionId, Step, Total, Message, Progress, Phase}

Phase values: creating_fsi, scanning_sources, loading_assemblies,
  opening_namespaces, finalizing

VS Code extension:
- Handle warmup_progress in LiveTestingListener callbacks
- Show phase-specific status bar text during warmup
- Clear warmup state on session ready

Neovim plugin (sagefs.nvim):
- Add warmup_progress to SSE classifier and event map
- Track warmup state in module for statusline display
- Show phase label in statusline during warmup
- Notify on major phase transitions
- Clear state on warmup_context_snapshot (session ready)

Visual Studio extension:
- Accept both session_warmup_progress and warmup_progress event types
- Show warmup phase details in StatusBarManager
- Format warmup message with step/total for namespace opens

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
